title: Will President Trump send ICE agents to Boston’s Logan Airport?
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: President Trump plans to send ICE agents to airports to arrest illegal immigrants unless DHS funding is approved.
Logan Airport's TSA staff remains stable while facing the federal shutdown, unlike other major US airports.
Massport reports no current impacts at Logan, but future staffing issues could necessitate ICE's involvement.

title: Is Bruins goalie Jeremy Swayman a legitimate Vezina candidate? The numbers speak for themselves.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman saved 42 of 44 shots in Bruins' 4-2 win over Red Wings, boosting playoff odds.
Swayman's .908 save percentage doesn't reflect his exceptional performance against high-danger scoring chances.
Swayman ranks second in NHL for goals saved above expected, justifying Vezina Trophy consideration.

title: Celtics legend Paul Pierce continues to make a case for Jaylen Brown as NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paul Pierce advocates for Celtics star Jaylen Brown's NBA MVP candidacy, praising his performance in Tatum's absence.
Jaylen Brown averages 28.5 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists this season, all career highs in 63 games.
Pierce attributes lack of MVP recognition for Brown to Boston's widespread success, causing potential animosity and underappreciation.

title: Plymouth police officer on paid leave amid criminal investigation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Plymouth Officer Samantha Pelrine on paid administrative leave amid a State Police criminal investigation into her off-duty conduct.
The investigation began last week, with Plymouth Police and Massachusetts State Police confirming its active, ongoing status.
Plymouth Police Department emphasizes accountability, conducting an internal investigation and adhering to policy during the process.

title: MLB writer predicts Roman Anthony will win AL MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: MLB writer Eno Sarris predicts Roman Anthony will win the AL MVP in the 2026 season.
Roman Anthony impressed in 2025 with a .292 average, 8 home runs, and 32 RBIs over 71 games.
Anthony batted .280 with 2 home runs for Team USA in the 2026 World Baseball Classic.

title: Pedestrian killed in Roxbury hit-and-run
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A pedestrian was killed in a hit-and-run Friday morning near Southampton and Bradston streets in Roxbury.
The victim was pronounced dead at a nearby hospital; police have located the vehicle involved in the incident.
No arrests have been made as the hit-and-run investigation remains ongoing, according to Boston police.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about chances for snow through Monday
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: National Weather Service predicts up to 2 inches of wet snow near I-90 by Monday night.
Rain is expected to transition to snow early Monday morning across northern MA, CT, RI, and SE MA.
Meteorologists forecast snow coatings up to 2 inches possible in localized areas with steadier snow bands.

title: Mass. police officer arrested for OUI, department says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Norwood police officer Shawn Willman arrested for operating under the influence; he was off duty at the time.
Willman placed on administrative leave, employment pending the outcome of the criminal case, according to Norwood Police.
An internal affairs investigation is underway, with no court information available for Willman as of Sunday evening.

title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, from Marston Mills, MA, charged with aggravated battery after allegedly stabbing friend in Miami Beach.
Incident occurred after a fight over women met at bars during a Miami Beach bachelor party, leading to a stabbing.
Dickerson's injuries and weapon linked him to crime; both men hospitalized, Dickerson held without bond.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution lost to St. Louis City SC 3-1, marking their third defeat in four games.
Revolution's Ilay Feingold scored first, but St. Louis responded with three unanswered goals to secure the win.
Forward Leo Campana exited the game with a new injury; details on its nature and severity are pending.
